title: Distributed tracing — W3C TRACEPARENT propagation through aho dispatcher and router; end-to-end Jaeger trace; Gemini OTEL asymmetry ADR

scope_notes.router_entry_point_clarification: Plan doc §W2.3 uses the phrase 'same treatment at route() entry' for the router module. The router module does not define a function named route(); the canonical classification entry is classify_task(). NemoClawOrchestrator.route() calls into classify_task. Per design doc §W2 module-level language ('Read TRACEPARENT env var in src/aho/pipeline/router.py'), wrapping classify_task is the correct reading. Recorded here so Gemini audit does not raise as drift. Confirmed with Kyle mid-implementation before bucket 5.
scope_notes.dispatcher_return_shape_delta: src/aho/pipeline/dispatcher.py dispatch() success paths now return two additional keys: tokens_eval (from Ollama eval_count) and tokens_prompt (from Ollama prompt_eval_count). Purely additive — no existing caller reads either key. Fields are used to populate dispatch.tokens_eval and dispatch.tokens_prompt span attributes without re-parsing the Ollama response body from within the span-wrapping logic. Closure-capture pattern (capturing the body via a shared variable instead of returning it) was considered but rejected: the closure approach tangles span-attribute population into the retry loop's many early-return points. Dict-extension keeps span logic separate from dispatch logic. Noted as a clean-follow-up candidate if the return contract ever needs tightening. Confirmed with Kyle mid-implementation.
scope_notes.in_process_parent_resolution_refinement: First probe run surfaced a subtle semantic issue: aho.dispatch and aho.route.classify spans were both parenting to the env TRACEPARENT, making them siblings under the Claude Code tool.execution span even when one was nested inside the other. Fixed via _resolve_span_parent_context() precedence: if an OTEL span is already active in the Python context, use it (nest in-process); otherwise fall back to env TRACEPARENT. After the fix, aho.dispatch.nemotron (called from inside classify_task) correctly appears as a child of aho.route.classify in the extracted sample trace. Backward-compat tests re-run clean post-refinement (76/76 in dispatcher+router zone). Applied before bucket 5 final probe capture.
scope_notes.probe_model_choice: End-to-end probe uses qwen3.5:9b as the producer (direct dispatch call) and nemotron-mini:4b as the classifier (via classify_task). Initial probe attempt used qwen for both; qwen thinking-mode blew the 30s router timeout, then the 180s router timeout, on a simple 3-category classification task. CLAUDE.md §Findings Carried Forward and 0.2.15 retrospective both explicitly state Nemotron is classifier/triage only and qwen thinking-mode eats num_predict on long prompts — the probe honored that assignment rather than force qwen through classify. Qwen was unload_model()'d before nemotron classify call to free VRAM on the 8GB GPU.

acceptance_checks.check: Traces beta enabled; all three OTEL pipelines (metrics, logs, traces) attribute aho.workstream=W2 literal within 60s of workstream_start
acceptance_checks.evidence: .claude/settings.json env block (modified pre-session by Kyle) now sets CLAUDE_CODE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA=1, OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER=otlp, OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES contains aho.workstream=W2. Session-start env verification: AHO_ITERATION=0.2.16, AHO_WORKSTREAM=W2, CLAUDE_CODE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA=1, OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER=otlp all confirmed via env check. workstream_start event emitted at 2026-04-23T23:49:23Z via `aho iteration workstream start W2`. Within 60s: logs.jsonl last record aho.workstream=W2 literal (not ${AHO_WORKSTREAM}, not W1); metrics.jsonl last record aho.workstream=W2 literal; traces.jsonl had resourceSpans record with aho.workstream=W2 literal at mtime 2026-04-23T16:50Z. No halt needed; the 'traces beta not taking effect' escape-hatch was not tripped. .aho-checkpoint.json updated: current_workstream=W2, W2=in_progress. last_event field still reads W1_workstream_complete; inspected W0→W1 transition evidence and confirmed this is existing wrapper behavior (last_event tracks terminal events, not starts) — not a W2 regression.

acceptance_checks.check: TRACEPARENT propagation in src/aho/pipeline/dispatcher.py — child span off parent when env present, root span when absent
acceptance_checks.evidence: Module-level tracer obtained via opentelemetry.trace.get_tracer('aho.pipeline.dispatcher'); TraceContextTextMapPropagator instantiated once at module load. _resolve_span_parent_context() reads env TRACEPARENT, extracts via propagator, returns OTEL Context. dispatch() signature extended with stage: Optional[str] = None (additive kwarg). Retry loop body wrapped in nested _run_dispatch() closure; closure invocation wrapped in `with _tracer.start_as_current_span(f'aho.dispatch.{family}', context=...) as span:`. Span name aho.dispatch.{family}. Attrs at entry: aho.model, aho.family, dispatch.num_ctx, dispatch.num_predict, aho.stage (when passed). Attrs at exit: dispatch.duration_ms (sourced from result['wall_clock_seconds']*1000 on success; from span-local monotonic delta on typed-error), dispatch.tokens_eval, dispatch.tokens_prompt. On DispatchError: span.record_exception + Status(ERROR) + dispatch.duration_ms before re-raise. On graceful-error result dict: Status(ERROR, result['error'][:200]). No leaked open spans. No except Exception. ImportError-only guard on the opentelemetry import so the module still imports if the SDK is unavailable. Verified via src/aho/pipeline/dispatcher.py lines 17-28 (imports), 220-249 (parent-context helper), 307-471 (dispatch() with span wrap).

acceptance_checks.check: TRACEPARENT propagation in src/aho/pipeline/router.py — classify_task wraps in aho.route.classify span; attrs from spec
acceptance_checks.evidence: Same module-level tracer + propagator pattern. INPUT_EXCERPT_MAX_CHARS = 200 defined as named constant (not inline magic number). classify_task() body wrapped in nested _run_classify() closure; closure invocation wrapped in `with _tracer.start_as_current_span('aho.route.classify', ...) as span:`. Attrs at entry: aho.classifier_model, aho.input_excerpt (task[:INPUT_EXCERPT_MAX_CHARS]). Attrs at exit: aho.category (matched category), aho.duration_ms (monotonic delta). On DispatchError: record_exception + Status(ERROR) + aho.duration_ms before re-raise. Verified via src/aho/pipeline/router.py lines 20-47 (imports + constants), 65-85 (parent-context helper), 97-188 (classify_task with span wrap). Router-entry wording clarification documented in scope_notes.

acceptance_checks.check: Backward compatibility — 6 new unit tests pass; existing dispatcher+router tests pass unchanged without TRACEPARENT set
acceptance_checks.evidence: artifacts/tests/test_dispatcher_traceparent.py (3 tests): test_absent_emits_root_span (asserts span.parent is None, span.name='aho.dispatch.qwen', dispatch.num_ctx+dispatch.duration_ms+dispatch.tokens_eval+dispatch.tokens_prompt attrs present); test_present_emits_child_span_inheriting_trace_id (asserts span.context.trace_id == parent trace_id int, span.parent.span_id == parent span_id int, aho.stage='producer' attr propagated); test_malformed_emits_root_span (malformed TRACEPARENT value → invalid propagator context → root span). artifacts/tests/test_router_traceparent.py (3 tests): absent→root, present→child (same trace_id + parent span_id assertions), long-input→excerpt truncated to INPUT_EXCERPT_MAX_CHARS exactly. Mock strategy: isolated TracerProvider with InMemorySpanExporter, patch.object(module, '_tracer', harness.tracer). urllib.request.urlopen mocked for dispatcher tests; router.dispatch mocked for router tests. No network. Existing regression: test_dispatcher_hardening.py (52 tests) + test_dispatcher_chat_api.py + test_dispatcher_template_leak.py + test_pipeline_router.py (24 tests) + 6 new = 89/89 pass.

acceptance_checks.check: End-to-end trace probe — single Jaeger trace captured covering Claude Code → tool_use → bash subprocess → aho.dispatch → Ollama
acceptance_checks.evidence: Probe script artifacts/iterations/0.2.16/probes/w2_end_to_end_probe.py invoked via bash subprocess from this session. TRACEPARENT inherited: 00-d4c162ab018f5f65d83b06fb65a125c8-f547485b93ccf097-01 (trace_id d4c162ab018f5f65d83b06fb65a125c8, parent span f547485b93ccf097). Probe emitted: (1) direct dispatcher.dispatch('qwen3.5:9b', ..., stage='w2-probe-producer') → aho.dispatch.qwen span 80de4ed8d2b69aca parent=f547485b93ccf097; (2) router.classify_task(..., model='nemotron-mini:4b') → aho.route.classify span 5a10d32601925680 parent=f547485b93ccf097, which internally dispatched to nemotron → aho.dispatch.nemotron span 5c5fe9cc4458d354 parent=5a10d32601925680 (correctly nested under route.classify after the in-process parent resolution refinement, not a sibling). Extracted subtree to artifacts/iterations/0.2.16/traces/end-to-end-sample.json: 5 spans (claude_code.tool → claude_code.tool.execution → aho.dispatch.qwen + aho.route.classify → aho.dispatch.nemotron), 2 resource attribute profiles, well-formed JSON document with metadata.hierarchy_summary. Full trace carries 105 spans across the entire Claude Code session; the extracted subtree is scoped to the probe's tool invocation and aho descendants. Raw records inspected directly from ~/.local/share/aho/traces/traces.jsonl per G-operating-stance 'raw response is ground truth', not via Jaeger UI.

acceptance_checks.check: dispatch.duration_ms span attr matches dispatcher's internal wall-clock timing (no drift)
acceptance_checks.evidence: Probe stdout recorded dispatch.internal_timing_ms=7430.11 for the direct producer dispatch. Extracted span attrs: dispatch.duration_ms=7430. Delta 0.11ms is measurement noise — sourced from the round(elapsed, 2) at seconds-to-ms conversion boundary. Implementation choice: dispatch.duration_ms is populated from result['wall_clock_seconds'] * 1000.0 on success, which is the same timing value that lands in the event log and stage artifacts — single source of truth, no two-measurement-site drift possible on the happy path. Classify: internal_timing_ms=1332.93; aho.route.classify span aho.duration_ms=1332.69; aho.dispatch.nemotron span dispatch.duration_ms=1330. Classify = nested dispatch + ~3ms span-wrapping overhead, as expected.

acceptance_checks.check: OTEL payload truncation — OTEL_LOG_TOOL_CONTENT=1 behavior observed and documented
acceptance_checks.evidence: artifacts/iterations/0.2.16/trace-integration-notes.md §Truncation documents the observation. In this harness config, tool_result records in logs.jsonl stay at 1.3-1.8KB regardless of actual tool_result_size_bytes — largest observed tool_result_size_bytes=259568 (mcp_tool), corresponding logs.jsonl record=1766 bytes. The 60KB documented truncation threshold doesn't visibly materialize because OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=file:~/.local/share/aho/api-bodies/ routes raw content to a file sink rather than inlining it in OTLP logs. Posture observation for Mercor reference-pack consumers: customers without the file sink should expect inline content up to 60KB; customers with the file sink see tool_result_size_bytes metadata only in logs and full content in the sink directory. No test asserts the truncation point — pipeline configuration determines where the cut materializes.

acceptance_checks.check: Gemini CLI OTEL asymmetry ADR written with next-sequential aho-internal ADR number
acceptance_checks.evidence: `command ls artifacts/adrs/` returned 0001-0004 as aho-internal sequence (0003 was W0 OTEL scaffolding posture, 0004 was W0 iteration-close-confirm redesign; ahomw-ADR-044/045 are kjtcom cross-project references, not the aho-internal sequence). Next sequential = 0005. File: artifacts/adrs/0005-gemini-otel-asymmetry.md. Decision: accept the asymmetry. No timing-wrapper half-measures. Harness-watcher event wrappers capture wall-clock only. Audit cost attribution remains drafter-only in the Pillar 8 dashboard. Rationale (verbatim from ADR): 'partial observability that looks like coverage it isn't is worse than clear documented asymmetry.' Three alternatives considered and rejected: wall-clock span wrapper (would visually suggest parity that doesn't exist); prompt-length-based token estimate (fabricated numeric precision corrupts Pillar 8 ground-truth contract); stream Gemini CLI stderr (fragile contract against a human-readable summary format). Revisit triggers documented. trace-integration-notes.md §Gemini CLI Asymmetry updated to reference 0005 by actual number post-enumeration. Cross-project contamination check: ADR number determined from disk enumeration (not fabricated, not from plan or design doc prediction); 0.2.15 left highest aho-internal at 0002, and two W0 ADRs (0003+0004) landed in 0.2.16 — enumerated numbers used verbatim.

acceptance_checks.check: Baseline regression — no new failures attributable to W2; W2 net-new passes count matches new tests added
acceptance_checks.evidence: Full artifacts/tests/ run (excluding test_nemoclaw_real.py + test_cascade_integration.py + test_live_models.py + test_glm_live.py live-model integration tests): 12 failed / 408 passed / 1 skipped / 421 collected / 839.80s. All 12 failing tests match entries in artifacts/harness/test-baseline.json exactly (test_daemon_healthy baseline entry flake-passed, not in failed list — same variance pattern as W1 close). Zero new failures attributable to W2 scope. Targeted regression (dispatcher+router zone, pre-baseline-run): 89/89 pass (52 hardening + 7 chat_api + 6 template_leak + 24 pipeline_router + 6 new traceparent) in 0.11s. Dispatcher+router zone is the only code W2 touched in src/aho/pipeline/; no regressions in that zone is the primary safety check. See test_results.baseline_regression.w1_close_baseline_for_comparison.notes for a discrepancy flag re: total-collected count.

acceptance_checks.check: Hygiene — pycache cleared after src/aho/pipeline/ edits (G070); only affected daemons restarted (G071)
acceptance_checks.evidence: find src/aho -name __pycache__ -type d | xargs rm -rf run after each dispatcher/router edit. systemctl --user list-units --type=service --state=running enumerated: aho-dashboard, aho-nemoclaw, aho-openclaw, aho-otel-collector, aho-telegram, aho-jaeger. grep check: only src/aho/agents/nemoclaw.py imports aho.pipeline.router (via 'from aho.pipeline.router import ...' at line 19). aho-dashboard/server.py, aho-openclaw, aho-telegram/notifications.py — zero pipeline.dispatcher / pipeline.router imports. Per-unit decision, not batch restart: aho-nemoclaw restarted twice (after initial router edit; after nesting-refinement edit). is-active verified =active both times. aho-dashboard/openclaw/telegram/jaeger/otel-collector left untouched.

acceptance_checks.check: Pillar 11 — no git operations performed by agent
acceptance_checks.evidence: Zero `git add`, `git commit`, `git push`, `git merge`, `git reset`, `git checkout` invocations by agent in this session. git diff and git status are read-only and used only for diff-size reporting. claude_code.commit.count and claude_code.pull_request.count in metrics.jsonl remain at 0 delta for this workstream — Pillar 11 monitored-invariant contract held. Secrets not read (~/.config/fish/config.fish not touched). .claude/settings.json modifications were Kyle-performed pre-session, not agent-performed. .aho-checkpoint.json transitioned via sanctioned `aho iteration workstream start W2` wrapper, not direct file edit.

acceptance_checks.check: G081 operating stance — no celebratory framing in archive or comments
acceptance_checks.evidence: Acceptance archive text, trace-integration-notes.md, ADR-0005, and new test files scanned for banned phrases 'landed beautifully', 'shipped cleanly', 'all green', 'clean close'. Zero matches. Evidence blocks state results directly with measurement precision, not rhetorical flourish. Deviations and open items flagged with same prominence as passing checks.

baseline_regression.baseline_reference_source: artifacts/harness/test-baseline.json (13 documented baseline failures; 1 flake-passing run-to-run: test_daemon_healthy::test_healthy_unit_passes)

w1_close_baseline_for_comparison.notes: W1 close recorded 12/408/1 (421 collected). W2 ran 12/408/1 (421 collected). Identical passed+failed+skipped pattern; all 12 failures match baseline entries exactly. W2 added 6 new tests (all passing) but the total-collected count also remained 421 — a worth-flagging discrepancy. Possibilities: (a) W1 close count already included pytest's collection of my new files which landed in this session's test dir at write-time; (b) some tests removed during W1 execution I'm not aware of. Not a W2 scope issue; flagging in audit record so Gemini can probe if desired. The zero-new-failures signal is the load-bearing check and holds regardless.

baseline_regression.failure_set_match: All 12 failing tests are in test-baseline.json entries: test_build_log_stub, test_postflight_residuals/run_types, test_report_builder x2, test_telegram_ws_commands x4, test_gate_verbosity, test_ws_fixes. test_daemon_healthy baseline entry flake-passed (not in failed list this run — same as W1-close variance). Zero failures attributable to W2 scope.

otel_telemetry_evidence.pipeline: Claude Code OTEL (metrics+logs+traces) → otelcol-contrib (gRPC :4317) → file exporters → ~/.local/share/aho/{metrics,logs,traces}/{metrics,logs,traces}.jsonl; aho dispatcher/router spans join via TRACEPARENT inherited from Claude Code tool span → OTLP/gRPC → same collector → traces.jsonl
otel_telemetry_evidence.duration_drift_check: dispatch.duration_ms span attr (7430) vs probe stdout dispatch.internal_timing_ms (7430.11): 0.11ms delta, consistent with the round(elapsed, 2) cast at seconds-to-ms boundary. aho.route.classify duration_ms (1332.69) vs probe stdout classify.internal_timing_ms (1332.93): 0.24ms delta. Both within measurement noise.
otel_telemetry_evidence.classify_category_note: Nemotron classifier mapped 'Write a Python function that computes fibonacci numbers.' to 'prose' rather than 'code'. Classifier conformance observation, not a W2 concern — trace propagation semantics are orthogonal to classifier quality. Flagged as a quality data-point for any future classifier-quality workstream; no action here.

Pillar count 11 verified against CLAUDE.md verbatim. ahomw-ADR-044/ahomw-ADR-045 recognized as kjtcom cross-project references and deliberately NOT counted in the aho-internal sequence. No 'v10.66', 'IAO Pillars', or other kjtcom-shape constructs drifted into W2 artifacts.

Dashboard daemon was not touched (it does not import pipeline.*); aho-nemoclaw restart was G071-mandatory after router edit, not unsanctioned state change. .aho-checkpoint.json transitioned via sanctioned `aho iteration workstream start W2` wrapper.

deviations_from_brief.deviation: Dispatcher return dict gained tokens_eval + tokens_prompt keys (contract extension, additive)
deviations_from_brief.reason: Span-attribute population pattern needed tokens visible at span-wrap exit without double-parsing the Ollama body. Confirmed with Kyle mid-implementation (answer: as done, proceed; document under scope_notes.dispatcher_return_shape_delta — done).

deviations_from_brief.deviation: Plan §W2.3 wording 'route() entry' reconciled to classify_task() as canonical router entry
deviations_from_brief.reason: router.py has no route() function. classify_task is the module entry per design doc §W2 wording ('Read TRACEPARENT env var in src/aho/pipeline/router.py'). Confirmed with Kyle mid-implementation (answer: as done, proceed; document under scope_notes.router_entry_point_clarification — done).

deviations_from_brief.deviation: In-process span parent resolution refined after first probe surfaced sibling-not-nested semantics
deviations_from_brief.reason: First probe captured aho.dispatch + aho.route.classify as siblings under tool.execution (both re-parenting to env TRACEPARENT). Refined _resolve_span_parent_context() to prefer active Python context when one exists. Not a scope expansion — the original design clearly intended nested aho spans; the first implementation just failed to honor active-context priority. Targeted tests re-run clean post-refinement.

carry_forward_candidates.item: F-W1-001 bin wrapper (aho workstream init W{N}) — write literal resource attrs, restart Claude Code
carry_forward_candidates.rationale: Documented in W1 acceptance as carry-forward; explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for W2 per Kyle's brief. Noted here for continuity so it doesn't get lost between workstreams.

carry_forward_candidates.item: Classifier quality data-point — nemotron-mini:4b mapped 'write a Python fibonacci function' to 'prose' in probe
carry_forward_candidates.rationale: Orthogonal to W2 trace propagation scope; captured as an observation only. If classifier conformance drift becomes a recurring theme, a dedicated probe + test-suite workstream would be the right container — not an ad-hoc fix inside unrelated work.

carry_forward_candidates.item: Closure-capture pattern for span-attribute population (alternative to dict-extension)
carry_forward_candidates.target: Any future iteration where dispatcher return contract needs tightening
carry_forward_candidates.rationale: Additive dict keys tokens_eval/tokens_prompt got the job done and keep span-wrap logic separate from dispatch core. If the return contract ever needs to shrink to a fixed schema, moving these fields out of the dict and into a closure-captured local would be the clean migration path. Noted per scope_notes.dispatcher_return_shape_delta and Kyle's confirmation message.

carry_forward_candidates.item: Collector OTLP alias deprecation warning (tracked from W0)
carry_forward_candidates.rationale: otelcol-contrib v0.149.0 prints 'otlp alias deprecated, use otlp_grpc' on the otlp/jaeger exporter. Unchanged in W2. Rename to otlp_grpc/jaeger whenever the collector config is next edited.
